title: parahebe raoulii subsp. maccaskillii
intro: Parahebe raoulii subsp. maccaskillii is a subspecies of a flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and has been used to treat various conditions, including skin irritations and respiratory issues. The herbal preparation of Parahebe raoulii subsp. maccaskillii typically involves drying the leaves and flowers, which can then be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
